description_text:
Position Overview

Data Analyst - Entry Level (Remote). We are looking for the right people who want to innovate, achieve, grow and lead. We attract and retain the best talent by investing in our employees and empowering them to develop themselves and their careers. Experience the challenges, rewards and opportunity of working for one of the world's largest providers of products and services to the global energy industry.

Key Responsibilities
• Perform data engineering, data modeling and model deployment under general supervision.
• Analyze large scale complex business data (time series data, structured/unstructured) from various data sources and draw insights.
• Leverage common open-source Machine Learning/Deep Learning packages for identifying data patterns and/or building predictive models.
• Conduct statistical analysis to determine trends and significant data relationships.
• Keep up to date with the latest Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence advancements.
• Work with data engineers to design and construct data pipelines for reproducible analysis.
• Leverage cloud computing technologies like Microsoft Azure and distributed computing technologies like Apache Spark.
• Present results of analyses, including design of graphs, charts, tables, and other data visualizations.

Required Qualifications
• Industry experience in predictive modeling, data science and analysis.
• Knowledge of Machine Learning frameworks and packages, including Keras, TensorFlow, Scikit-Learn and cloud computing platforms like Azure.
• Experience handling terabyte size datasets, diving into data to discover hidden patterns and using data visualization tools.
• Experience writing code in Python, R, Scala, and distributed computing technologies like Spark.
• Demonstrated teamwork, strong communication skills, and collaboration in complex engineering projects.
• Completion of an undergraduate degree in STEM.

Preferred Qualifications
• Master's degree in STEM is preferred.
• Candidates with qualifications exceeding the minimum requirements will be considered for higher level roles.

Who We Are
Join a team that puts its People First! Since 1889, First American (NYSE: FAF) has held an unwavering belief in its people. They are passionate about what they do, and we are equally passionate about fostering an environment where all feel welcome, supported, and empowered to be innovative and reach their full potential. Our inclusive, people-first culture has earned our company numerous accolades, including being named to the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For® list for ten consecutive years. We have also earned awards as a best place to work for women, diversity and LGBTQ+ employees, and have been included on more than 50 regional best places to work lists. First American will always strive to be a great place to work, for all. For more information, please visit www.careers.firstam.com.

What We Do
Our Technology team is at the forefront of this transformation, working tirelessly to modernize the real estate title and closing process that leverage Data Science solutions.

This summer, we're thrilled to offer internship programs to university students based near our Southern California headquarters, or anywhere in the continental USA. If you've ever considered a career in technology, this is a fantastic opportunity to gain hands-on experience.

Come join an exciting and transformative organization where the sky is the limit! By choice, we don't simply accept individuality - we embrace it, support, and thrive on it. In addition to focusing on you, our People First Culture celebrates collective diversity, equity, and inclusion not simply because it's the right thing to do but also because we believe it's the key to our success. We are proud to foster an authentic and inclusive workplace for all. First American is an equal-opportunity employer.

The types of things you'll do

Data Science is a broad area with an extensive opportunity space. Each intern will have their unique mission refined before the start date, after an offer is extended and accepted. Here are some of the value adds you'll be working on:
• Design data science solutions using industry-leading tools and platforms to drive business impact.
• Collaborate with the core team to assist in writing unit tests and scripts.
• Develop scripts for automated experiment tracking, comparison, and root cause analysis (RCA).
• Work with the Data Engineering team to build event-driven, user journey super datasets.
• Perform clustering analysis on task sequences to identify common workflows and outlier patterns.
• Leverage Generative AI and classical Machine Learning models for tasks such as document extraction and classification.
• Create automated scripts and models to reconcile and consolidate experimental results datasets. Explore and develop quantitative strategies for creating "Golden/Truth" datasets to be used for model testing and benchmarking.
Basic Qualifications
• Currently pursuing a bachelor's or master's degree at an accredited university.
• Majoring in quantitative disciplines, such as Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Operations Research, Applied Mathematics, Electrical Engineering.
• Prior exposure to best practices through hackathons, design challenges, university projects, or previous internships.
• Available full-time, 8-10 weeks for internship starting in early June 2026 and ending in August/September 2026.

What's in it for you?
• Competitive Pay: We offer competitive industry pay for our internship program.
• Industry Experience: The rare opportunity to impact one of the most prominent players in the United States real estate market.
• Mentorship & Buddy Program: We're in this together. You will be coached by an experienced mentor and paired with a buddy to answer your questions along the way.
• Networking: A chance to build your network with intelligent and hardworking interns and First American employees who share your passion for technology.
• Exposure: Access to cutting-edge tools, learning and development resources, and the opportunity to present your project at our Intern Expo Day in front of First American leadership and fellow interns.
• Extended Opportunities: Possibility of continuing part-time with our work study internship program or joining our First American team as a full-time early career hire.

$40.00 - $45.00 hourly

This hiring range is a reasonable estimate of the base pay range for this position at the time of posting. Pay is based on a number of factors which may include job-related knowledge, skills, experience, business requirements and geographic location.
• * Note that the following statements only apply to candidates who will be working from an unincorporated area within Los Angeles County. **

First American will consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with arrest or conviction records, in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable state and local laws (e.g., the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act).

First American intends to conduct a review of an applicant's criminal history in connection with a conditional offer. First American reasonably believes that a criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following material job duties for this position potentially resulting in the withdrawal of the conditional offer of employment: handling of confidential, proprietary or trade secret information belonging to First American or its customers, administrating or facilitating financial transactions, and the ability to meet customer-imposed criminal history requirements.

What We Offer
By choice, we don't simply accept individuality - we embrace it, we support it, and we thrive on it! Our People First Culture celebrates diversity, equity and inclusion not simply because it's the right thing to do, but also because it's the key to our success. We are proud to foster an authentic and inclusive workplace For All. You are free and encouraged to bring your entire, unique self to work. First American is an equal opportunity employer in every sense of the term.

Based on eligibility, First American offers a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, 401k, PTO/paid sick leave and other great benefits like an employee stock purchase plan.

Position Overview

Position: Business Data Analyst, Construction. We are one of the leading mechanical contractors in the eastern United States. Our construction and service (mechanical and electrical) experience spans commercial and government projects including data centers, hospitals, sports stadiums, convention centers, hotels, and research laboratories. The Business Data Analyst assists the company in meeting its objectives by supporting management and providing analysis of business data for effective decision making and organizational efficiency improvement. Location: Baltimore, MD 21227. Reports to: Vice President. FLSA Status: Full-Time / Exempt. Work Shift: 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. / Mon. – Fri.

Key Responsibilities
• Aggregates and performs statistical analysis on business data based on requests from project teams or senior leadership.
• Integrates data from multiple sources or functional areas, ensuring data accuracy and integrity, and updates data as needed.
• Writes queries or reports to provide data for various analytical projects and interprets data to identify business trends and conduct root cause analysis.
• Generates analytical and statistical information in various formats using advanced features of Microsoft Excel, Microsoft Power BI, and related software.
• Complies with all company operating policies, procedures, and safety programs as established.

Preferred Qualifications
• Bachelor of Science degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, and/or a related field.
• One to three years of professional experience, including internship experience.
• Advanced experience in Microsoft Office Excel and Power BI applications.
• Familiarity with software applications utilized in data analytics for business.
• Must possess professional integrity and be trustworthy.
• Strong interpersonal communication skills to effectively collaborate with internal and external stakeholders.
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
• Proven ability to organize and manage multiple projects.
• Ability to independently analyze and solve problems on both strategic and tactical levels.
• Commitment to company values.
• Effective communication, cooperation, and collaboration with employees, government agencies, contractors, vendors, and/or suppliers.

Position Overview

SynergisticIT has been transforming careers since 2010 by matching talented candidates with top technology clients such as Apple, Google, and more. In today’s challenging job market, entry-level professionals in software development and data science must demonstrate exceptional skills and hands-on project experience to perform from day one. All positions are open to US citizens and candidates holding any visa. Visa Sponsorship assistance including STEM extension, H1B, and Green Card filing is provided.

Key Responsibilities
• Demonstrate industry-relevant skills on real-world projects from day one.
• Engage in rigorous interview preparation and hands-on project work.
• Collaborate effectively with teams and contribute to client deliverables.
• Participate in optional Skill and Technology Enhancement Programs to bridge any skill gaps.
• Uphold high performance standards that meet diverse client requirements.

Required Qualifications
• For Software Development Roles:
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Information Systems, or IT.
• Proficiency in Java, Core Java, JavaScript, C++, or related programming languages.
• Experience with Spring Boot, Microservices, Docker, Jenkins, and REST APIs.
• Highly motivated, self-driven with excellent written and verbal communication skills.
• For Data Science / Machine Learning Roles:
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field.
• Experience with Python, SAS, data visualization tools and familiarity with Computer Vision.
• Strong foundation in Statistics combined with hands-on project work in data analytics.
• Highly motivated with excellent verbal and written communication skills.

Preferred Qualifications
• For Data Science / Machine Learning roles: Experience with NLP, Text Mining, Tableau, PowerBI and Time Series Analysis.

Benefits & Perks
• Visa Sponsorship Assistance including filing for STEM extension, H1B, and Green Card.
• Skill and Technology Enhancement Programs available for candidates needing additional industry exposure.
• Opportunities to work with leading technology clients such as Apple, Google, Paypal, and more.
• Full-time positions with immediate client engagement.

At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com.

As guided by Our Credo, Johnson & Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson & Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

Job Function:
Career Programs

Job Sub Function:
Non-LDP Intern/Co-Op

Job Category:
Career Program

All Job Posting Locations:
Spring House, Pennsylvania, United States of America

Job Description:

About Innovative Medicine

Our expertise in Innovative Medicine is informed and inspired by patients, whose insights fuel our science-based advancements. Visionaries like you work on teams that save lives by developing the medicines of tomorrow.

Join us in developing treatments, finding cures, and pioneering the path from lab to life while championing patients every step of the way.

Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/innovative-medicine

About MedTech

Fueled by innovation at the intersection of biology and technology, we’re developing the next generation of smarter, less invasive, more personalized treatments.

Your unique talents will help patients on their journey to wellness. Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/medtech

We are searching for the best talent for Data Science - Multi-Omics intern to be in PA Spring House.
• The Intern / Co-op term is from June to August, 2026.
• Full time requirement (40 hours per week).
• Purpose:

We are seeking a highly motivated and innovative Data Science Intern to support our research and development initiatives focusing on CAR-T Therapy. This internship offers a unique opportunity to work with our AI/ML team on developing develop a multimodal, patient-level scorecard that summarizes immune and CAR-T product characteristics by integrating data from CITE-seq, drug product analytics, as well as flow cytometry and bulk profiling approaches. By effectively designing this framework, we aim to strengthen our decision-making processes

Key Responsibilities:
• Collaborate with the AI/ML team to develop and implement multimodal learning frameworks.
• Analyze and aggregate different modalities through biological insights and maximize benefits from multimodal learning.
• Present analytical findings to cross-functional teams, contextualizing results within broader project goals.
• Draft technical papers for high impact conferences and/or journals
Qualifications / Requirements:
• Completion of Undergraduate Freshman year at an accredited University is required.
• Currently pursuing or recently completed a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, bioinformatics and computational biology or a related field.
• Have a cumulative GPA of 2.8 or higher, which is reflective of all college coursework.
• Extensive hands-on experience in AI/ML research and applications and or experience with CITE-seq and scRNA-seq assay analysis.
• Experience in medical transcriptomics is a plus.
• Strong publication record
• Proficiency in Python, and experience with AI frameworks like PyTorch or TensorFlow.
• Python programming, numpy, pandas, scipy, scverse
• Strong communication skills to effectively communicate and collaborate in a cross-functional setting

Permanently authorized to work in the U.S., must not require sponsorship of an employment visa (e.g., H-1B or green card) at the time of application or in the future. Students currently on CPT, OPT, or STEM OPT usually requires future sponsorship for long term employment and do not meet the requirements for this program unless eligible for an alternative long-term status that does not require company sponsorship.

The expected pay range for this position is between $23.00 per hour and $51.50 per hour but will be based on candidate's program year, discipline, degree and/or experience. Co-Ops/Interns are eligible to participate in Company sponsored employee medical benefits in accordance with the terms of the plan. Co-Ops and Interns are eligible for the following sick time benefits: up to 40 hours per calendar year; for employees who reside in the State of Washington, up to 56 hours per calendar year. Co-Ops and Interns are eligible to participate in the Company’s consolidated retirement plan (pension).

For additional general information on Company benefits, please go to: https://www.careers.jnj.com/employee-benefits. This job posting is anticipated to close on [03/12/2026]. The Company may however extend this time-period, in which case the posting will remain available on https://www.careers.jnj.com to accept additional applications.

Ineligibility for severance.

Johnson & Johnson is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by federal, state or local law. We actively seek qualified candidates who are protected veterans and individuals with disabilities as defined under VEVRAA and Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act.

Johnson & Johnson is committed to providing an interview process that is inclusive of our applicants’ needs. If you are an individual with a disability and would like to request an accommodation, please contact us via https://www.jnj.com/contact-us/careers or contact AskGS to be directed to your accommodation resource.

Remote work options may be considered on a case-by-case basis and if approved by the Company.

Brisbane, California, United States of America, Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States of America, La Jolla, California, United States of America, Spring House, Pennsylvania, United States of America, Titusville, New Jersey, United States of America

Job Description:

About Innovative Medicine

Our expertise in Innovative Medicine is informed and inspired by patients, whose insights fuel our science-based advancements. Visionaries like you work on teams that save lives by developing the medicines of tomorrow.

Join us in developing treatments, finding cures, and pioneering the path from lab to life while championing patients every step of the way.

Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/innovative-medicine

About MedTech

Fueled by innovation at the intersection of biology and technology, we’re developing the next generation of smarter, less invasive, more personalized treatments.

Your unique talents will help patients on their journey to wellness. Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/medtech

We are searching for the best talent for Data Science Intern, Clinical Omics to be in PA Spring House.
• The Intern / Co-op term is from June to August, 2026.
• Full time requirement (40 hours per week).
Purpose:

We are seeking a highly motivated and innovative Data Science Intern to support research and development efforts focusing on the Clinical Omics Framework. This internship offers a unique opportunity to collaborate with the Population Analytics & Insights team to develop pathway enrichment modules for clinical proteomics data. These enhancements will directly inform biomarker strategies and support data-driven clinical trial decision-making across multiple therapeutic areas.

Key Responsibilities
• Contribute to the development and optimization of analysis modules used to support clinical trial decision-making.
• Collaborate with data scientists to refine workflows that integrate proteomics, clinical endpoints, and biological interpretation.
• Develop pathway enrichment approaches tailored to SomaScan and Olink proteomic platforms.
• Incorporate causal mediation analysis methods into the clinical omics framework.
• Evaluate how treatment interventions may influence clinical outcomes through intermediate molecular changes.
Qualifications / Requirements:
• Completion of Undergraduate Freshman year at an accredited University is required.
• Enrolled in an accredited college/university pursuing a PhD degree in Computational Biology, Human Genetics, Bioinformatics, Biostatistics, or a related field
• Be in your second year or above in school with a graduation date after August 2026
• GPA of 3.0 or above
• Authorized to work in the United States during the full duration of the summer internship (05/2026 – 08/2026)
• Familiarity with basic statistics concepts/terminology
• Detail-oriented, highly organized and able to manage multiple tasks
• Demonstrated ability to work independently as well as on a team
• Proficiency with Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Power Point) and have strong oral and written communication skills

Preferred Qualifications
• Experience with proteomics data (SomaScan or Olink preferred)
• Familiarity with pathway enrichment, mediation analysis, or causal inference methods
• Programming (e.g. R, Python, etc.) and data analytics experience is strongly preferred

Permanently authorized to work in the U.S., must not require sponsorship of an employment visa (e.g., H-1B or green card) at the time of application or in the future. Students currently on CPT, OPT, or STEM OPT usually requires future sponsorship for long term employment and do not meet the requirements for this program unless eligible for an alternative long-term status that does not require company sponsorship.

The expected pay range for this position is between $23.00 per hour and $51.50 per hour but will be based on candidate's program year, discipline, degree and/or experience. Co-Ops/Interns are eligible to participate in Company sponsored employee medical benefits in accordance with the terms of the plan. Co-Ops and Interns are eligible for the following sick time benefits: up to 40 hours per calendar year; for employees who reside in the State of Washington, up to 56 hours per calendar year. Co-Ops and Interns are eligible to participate in the Company’s consolidated retirement plan (pension).

For additional general information on Company benefits, please go to: https://www.careers.jnj.com/employee-benefits. This job posting is anticipated to close on [03/13/2026]. Job Function:
Career Programs

Non-LDP Intern/Co-Op

Job Category:
Career Program

All Job Posting Locations:
Titusville, New Jersey, United States of America

Job Description:

About Innovative Medicine

Our expertise in Innovative Medicine is informed and inspired by patients, whose insights fuel our science-based advancements. Visionaries like you work on teams that save lives by developing the medicines of tomorrow.

Join us in developing treatments, finding cures, and pioneering the path from lab to life while championing patients every step of the way.

Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/innovative-medicine

About MedTech

Fueled by innovation at the intersection of biology and technology, we’re developing the next generation of smarter, less invasive, more personalized treatments.

Your unique talents will help patients on their journey to wellness. Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/medtech

Position Components

In this role, you will work with colleagues in the JNJ Innovative Medicine R&D Data Science & Digital Health organization and partner with key businesses across JNJ Innovative Medicine (formerly Janssen) to apply advanced data science and analytics to answer key research questions. As an intern, you will get the opportunity to work on cutting-edge problems at the intersection of AI and healthcare. You will be a member of a dynamic team comprising data scientists and subject matter experts to create and iterate on data science solutions. Projects include, but are not limited to, use of real-world data for modeling and prediction of disease severity and progression, discovery of disease subtypes, risk stratification, and precision medicine using traditional biostatistical methodologies and advanced methodologies from machine learning and/or deep learning. Interns could be located out of our US locations in San Diego, San Francisco, Titusville, Raritan, Spring House, Boston as well as remote.

Minimum qualifications:
• Currently pursuing a PhD degree in Statistics, Biostatistics, Data Science, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Computational Biology, Biomedical Informatics, or related quantitative discipline.
• Strong working knowledge of AI and machine learning.
• Proficiency in Python and pytorch.
• Strong skills in predictive modeling and analysis of large datasets.
• Strong technical communication and presentation skills.

Preferred qualifications:

Experience in one or more of the following :
• Experience with processing and analyzing sensor or wearable device data.
• Experience with semi-supervised and/or self-supervised learning.
• Experience with Foundation Models

Join Our Team:

As part of our Cinemark Universe, you will immerse yourself in a fun and fast-paced environment that offers rewarding learning experiences. With 500+ theatres and nearly 6,000 screens; we're truly a global presence of 20,000 movie lovers working together to make unforgettable experiences. 

2026 Summer Internship Program: 
Are you ready to launch your career in entertainment and make a real impact? Cinemark’s Corporate Internship Program is your gateway to hands-on experience, professional development, and meaningful connections – all from our dynamic and inclusive corporate office. Join us for the 2026 Summer Internship, taking place June 1st to August 7th at Cinemark’s Service Center in Plano, TX.

What’s in it for you?
• Meaningful Contributions: You’ll work on impactful projects that align with real business goals, giving you the opportunity to make a visible difference. You’ll gain hands-on experience and exposure to the inner workings of a publicly traded company with a global footprint.
• Mentorship & Support: You will be paired with a dedicated peer coach to guide your development and help you navigate the experience.
• Executive Exposure: The program culminates in a final presentation to Cinemark’s executive leadership – your chance to showcase your work and insights to key decision-makers.
• Fun & Engaging Events: Build lasting connections through social activities, development workshops, and exclusive opportunities to engage with senior leaders.
• Full-Time Immersion: A 40-hour workweek during standard business hours gives you a true feel for a professional work environment.
• Hybrid Flexibility: Enjoy a hybrid schedule with 3 days onsite and 2 days remote, designed to balance collaboration and flexibility.
• Competitive Pay: Earn a competitive hourly wage while gaining valuable industry experience and professional development.

The role of a Data Science Intern:

We’re hiring two curious, data‑driven Pricing & Data Science Interns to help unlock insights and shape smarter pricing decisions. You’ll dive into real datasets, contribute to pricing strategy, support operational execution, and sharpen competitive intelligence – partnering with peer coaches and cross‑functional teams across Admission Tickets Pricing, Food & Beverage Pricing, and Competitive Intelligence.

Responsibilities:
• Gather, clean, and preprocess data from multiple sources.
• Analyze trends and correlate performance with market and demographic drivers.
• Assist with scripting/ automation /operation workflow to reduce manual effort and improve reliability.
• Support AI automation via data preparation, prototyping, QA, and data collection
• Create dashboards in Power BI to visualize trends, KPIs, and insights.
• Prepare concise summaries and recommendations for stakeholders.

Requirements:
• Current Junior or Senior pursuing a degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Economics, Business, Engineering, or a related field at an accredited university.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; curiosity and willingness to learn.
• Basic knowledge of data analysis tools, including Power BI, Python, SQL, and Excel.
• Exposure to LLMs/ GenAI, prompt engineering, or model evaluation a nice-to-have.
• Strong communication and presentation skills.
• Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

Here’s what our 2025 Interns had to say:
• "Through this internship, over the past ten weeks, I have been fortunate to experience incredible opportunities to grow both professionally and personally. I worked alongside outstanding people and gained valuable technical skills that I will carry throughout my career. I’m leaving this summer with lasting memories, meaningful relationships, and excitement for what comes next.” Cason Wetherill, Food & Beverage Inventory Intern
• “Over the past couple of months, I’ve grown tremendously—developing both technical and soft skills—while working in such a fun, welcoming, and enriching environment that truly made all the difference. I’m beyond grateful for the friendships built along the way and the memories I’ll carry forward. It’s safe to say that during my time here, I’ve watched more movies and eaten more popcorn than ever before—definitely the best “work perks” I could ask for.” Nicole Perdomo, Corporate Finance Intern
• “This internship has been one of the best professional development opportunities of my life. I’ve met some incredible people here, done some great work for my team, and learned a number of valuable skills. There’s more complexity to the movie distribution business than many people know- but Cinemark is a shining example of how to do it right. Many thanks for the good people of Cinemark for providing this absolutely CINEMATIC opportunity!” Stephan Norquist, Data Science Intern

At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com.

Job Description:

About Us:

Johnson & Johnson Innovation Medicine focuses on discovering and developing innovative solutions to address critical unmet medical needs across multiple therapeutic areas.

We are seeking a highly motivated and innovative Data Science Intern to support our research and development initiatives focusing on multimodality. This internship offers a unique opportunity to work with our AI/ML team on building a multimodal framework based on high dimension biology data, thereby driving the development of an innovative application. By effectively designing this framework, we aim to enhance our understanding of the materials utilized in our projects, strengthening decision-making processes and advancing drug discovery efforts.

Key Responsibilities:
• Collaborate with the AI/ML team to develop and implement multimodal learning frameworks.
• Analyze and aggregate different modalities through biological insights and maximize benefits from multimodal learning.
• Present analytical findings to cross-functional teams, contextualizing results within broader project goals.
• Draft technical papers for high impact conferences and/or journals

Qualifications:
• Currently pursuing or recently completed a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, or a related field.
• Extensive hands-on experience in AI/ML research and applications including Computer Vision, NLP, Geometry learning, or related fields.
• Experience in medical imaging and/or transcriptomics is a plus.
• Strong publication record (CVPR, ICCV, ICLR, NeurIPS, ICML etc.)
• Proficiency in Python, and experience with AI frameworks like PyTorch or TensorFlow.
• Strong communication skills to effectively communicate and collaborate in a cross-functional setting

Job Description:

About Innovative Medicine

Our expertise in Innovative Medicine is informed and inspired by patients, whose insights fuel our science-based advancements. Visionaries like you work on teams that save lives by developing the medicines of tomorrow.

Join us in developing treatments, finding cures, and pioneering the path from lab to life while championing patients every step of the way.

Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/innovative-medicine

About MedTech

Fueled by innovation at the intersection of biology and technology, we’re developing the next generation of smarter, less invasive, more personalized treatments.

Your unique talents will help patients on their journey to wellness. Learn more at https://www.jnj.com/medtech

We are searching for the best talent for Data Science Intern, PAI to be in PA Spring House.
• The Intern / Co-op term is from June to August, 2026.
• Full time requirement (40 hours per week).
• Purpose:

In this role, you will join the Data Science and Digital Health organization within Johnson and Johnson Innovative Medicine, focusing on leveraging large scale single cell transcriptomic data in the context of Neuroscience.
You will be responsible

·Collaborate and work closely with Data Scientists and Scientists to investigate disease associated cellular states and derive biological insights

·Perform advanced analytics to investigate how disease risk variants alter cellular states

·Apply single cell methods to analyze cellular state trajectories with respect to clinical disease progression and disease-related endophenotypes

·Follow all best practices for code version control and reproducible research
Qualifications / Requirements:
• Completion of Undergraduate Freshman year at an accredited University is required.
• Currently Enrolled in a PhD program in Data Science, Computational Biology, Bioinformatics, or related field
• Have a cumulative GPA of 2.8 or higher, which is reflective of all college coursework.
• 2+ years of hands-on experience in single cell transcriptomic analyses using Python-based methods
• Fluent in Python
• Experience with code version control using Git
• Preferred Qualifications:
• Solid foundation in Neuroscience single cell analytics and modeling
• Strong background in Neuroscience cell type biology
• Strong communication and scientific presentation skills
• Exposure to cellular state detection and analyses

Permanently authorized to work in the U.S., must not require sponsorship of an employment visa (e.g., H-1B or green card) at the time of application or in the future. Students currently on CPT, OPT, or STEM OPT usually requires future sponsorship for long term employment and do not meet the requirements for this program unless eligible for an alternative long-term status that does not require company sponsorship.

The expected pay range for this position is between $23.00 per hour and $51.50 per hour but will be based on candidate's program year, discipline, degree and/or experience. Co-Ops/Interns are eligible to participate in Company sponsored employee medical benefits in accordance with the terms of the plan. Co-Ops and Interns are eligible for the following sick time benefits: up to 40 hours per calendar year; for employees who reside in the State of Washington, up to 56 hours per calendar year. Co-Ops and Interns are eligible to participate in the Company’s consolidated retirement plan (pension).

For additional general information on Company benefits, please go to: https://www.careers.jnj.com/employee-benefits. This job posting is anticipated to close on 03/12/2026]. The Company may however extend this time-period, in which case the posting will remain available on https://www.careers.jnj.com to accept additional applications.

Ineligibility for severance.

Johnson & Johnson is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability, protected veteran status or other characteristics protected by federal, state or local law. We actively seek qualified candidates who are protected veterans and individuals with disabilities as defined under VEVRAA and Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act.

Johnson & Johnson is committed to providing an interview process that is inclusive of our applicants’ needs. If you are an individual with a disability and would like to request an accommodation, please contact us via https://www.jnj.com/contact-us/careers or contact AskGS to be directed to your accommodation resource.

Position Overview

Since 2010, Synergisticit has empowered job seekers in the tech market by providing the necessary skills, experience, and technical competence to excel at interviews and secure positions with top technology clients. We don’t just help you get a tech job – we help you build a career. Our candidates are achieving multiple job offers and $100k+ salaries, while working with industry giants such as Apple, Google, Paypal, and Walmart Labs.

We are currently looking for entry-level Software Programmers, Java Full-Stack Developers, Python/Java Developers, Data Analysts/Data Scientists, and Machine Learning Engineers for full-time roles. This opportunity is ideal for recent Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or Science graduates – or those looking to switch careers or re-enter the workforce.

Key Responsibilities
• Participate in technical projects and collaborate with top tech companies.
• Develop and apply programming skills using languages such as Java and Python.
• Engage with the job placement team and assist in matching candidate skills with client requirements.
• Demonstrate strong problem-solving and technical competencies during the interview and project phases.
• Leverage cutting-edge technologies to support client initiatives.

Required Qualifications
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Information Systems, or related fields.
• Demonstrated project work and practical experience in relevant technologies.
• Highly motivated, self-learner with excellent written and verbal communication skills.
• For Data Science/Machine Learning roles: Familiarity with Statistics, Gen AI, LLM, Python, Computer Vision, and data visualization tools.
• For Java Full-Stack/Software roles: Proficiency in Core Java, JavaScript, C++, Spring Boot, Microservices, Docker, Jenkins, GitHub, Kubernetes, and REST APIs.

Preferred Qualifications
• For Data Science/Machine Learning roles: Experience with NLP, Text Mining, Tableau, PowerBI, Databricks, or TensorFlow.
• For Java roles: Proven experience with software development tools and methodologies.

Benefits & Perks
• Visa Filing Assistance: Support with STEM extension, H1B, and Green Card applications.
• Opportunities to achieve multiple job offers and $100k+ salaries.
• Open positions for all visas and US citizens.
• Comprehensive career growth and job placement support.

Position Overview

In today’s challenging market—witnessing nearly 300,000 tech layoffs since October 2022—jobseekers must differentiate themselves to secure client interviews. At SynergisticIT, we don’t just focus on getting you a job; we build careers. Since 2010, we have empowered candidates by providing the requisite technical skills and industry experience to perform from day one. We work with top technology clients such as Apple, Google, PayPal, Visa, Walmart Labs, and more. Our open positions cater to entry-level software programmers, Java Full Stack developers, Python/Java developers, Data Analysts/Data Scientists, and Machine Learning engineers, and are available to all visas and US citizens.

Required Qualifications
• For Software/Full Stack/Programming Positions:
• Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Information Systems, or IT.
• Highly motivated, self-learner, and technically inquisitive.
• Experience in Java programming and understanding of the software development life cycle.
• Project work demonstrating skills in Core Java, JavaScript, C++, Spring Boot, Microservices, Docker, Jenkins, and REST APIs.
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
• For Data Science/Machine Learning Positions:
• Bachelor's or Master's degree in relevant fields like Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or Science.
• Hands-on project work in applicable technologies.
• Familiarity with Statistics, SAS, Python, Computer Vision, and data visualization tools.
• Highly motivated and self-driven with excellent communication skills.

Preferred Qualifications
• NLP
• Text Mining
• Tableau
• PowerBI
• TensorFlow

Benefits & Perks
• Assistance with STEM extension, H1B, and Green Card filings to eligible candidates.
• Optional skill and technology enhancement programs for those seeking to bridge skill gaps or gain client project experience.
• Access to comprehensive interview preparation resources and direct interactions with top tech companies.
• Opportunities to work with leading clients where you can contribute from day one.
• All positions are open for all visas and US citizens.
